words.definition: A set of rules or instructions that a computer follows to solve a problem or perform a task.
words.example: The search engine uses a complex algorithm to rank websites.

words.definition: The use of technology to perform tasks with minimal human intervention.
words.example: Automation in manufacturing has led to increased efficiency and reduced costs.

words.definition: A prejudice for or against one thing, person, or group compared with another, usually in a way considered to be unfair. In AI, it refers to systematic errors or prejudices in data or algorithms that lead to unfair outcomes.
words.example: Researchers are working to identify and mitigate bias in AI systems to ensure fairness.

words.definition: Relating to the mental processes of perception, memory, judgment, and reasoning.
words.example: Artificial intelligence aims to replicate human cognitive abilities.

words.definition: To move troops or equipment into position for military action; to bring resources into effective action.
words.example: The company plans to deploy its new AI system across all departments.

words.definition: A computing system inspired by the structure and function of the human brain, used in machine learning.
words.example: Deep learning models often utilize complex neural networks to process large datasets.

words.definition: Strong and unlikely to fail; (of a system or machine) able to withstand or overcome adverse conditions.
words.example: The AI system needs to be robust enough to handle unexpected inputs.

words.definition: To imitate the appearance or character of; to produce a computer model of.
words.example: Researchers use simulations to test the effectiveness of new AI models.
